mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for at least one month, the cost of capital rises sharply, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. Higher borrowing costs reduce corporate profitability and consumer spending capacity.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE change and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, jointly driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures broad-based economic strength that benefits equity valuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when both RSAFS and INDPRO show month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% quarter-over-quarter, signaling weakening residential investment and forward-looking consumer demand contraction. This leading indicator produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as growth expectations reset downward.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 52% of instances following a >10% HOUST decline, or the lagged correlation is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50,000 net new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, indicating labor market slack and reduced wage pressure. This combination signals lower inflation risk and potential monetary easing, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 50k AND UNRATE stable (±0.1pp)
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 4.0%, indicating real rates are insufficiently restrictive. This policy lag produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets price in imminent rate hikes and inflation persistence.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag in inflationary regime
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expands at an annualized rate above 3% for two consecutive months, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains. This supply-side strength supports profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO annualized growth > 3% for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with productivity gains
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate while retail sales (RSAFS) contract month-over-month, indicating synchronized demand weakness across consumption and overall economic activity. This dual contraction produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession risk rises.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS MoM < 0%
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances.
